transcript: Speaker A: What are you doing? Speaker B: I can't sleep. Speaker A: Do you mean: 'I can't fall asleep'? Speaker B: Yes, I can't fall asleep. Now 12 o'clock! Speaker A: You should say: 'It's 12 o'clock' or 'It's midnight'. Speaker B: Aha, okay! It's midnight. Speaker A: Great job! We can talk until you feel sleepy. Speaker B: Cool, but my English not good. Speaker A: Do you mean: 'My English isn't good'? Speaker B: All right, my English isn't good. Speaker A: Perfect correction! Don't worry. If you practice with me every day on the Loora app, I'll help fix your mistakes and you'll be confident in no time. Speaker B: Okay, I'll download Loora and see you tomorrow!

summary: An AI avatar corrects a user's English grammar mistakes in real-time during a casual bedtime conversation. The ad demonstrates how the Loora app helps users build speaking confidence through daily practice. The user concludes by promising to download the app.
